dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 responding for Falls.
Hi ma'am, I'm Alex, an EMT with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 today?
I fell down the stairs about 30 minutes ago and have severe pain in my left hip.
Can you tell me your name and where we are, please?
She's alert and oriented to person, place, and time.
GCS is 15: Eyes 4, Verbal 5, Motor 6. She's AOX4.
Your airway is clear and patent. Let me check your breathing.
Respiratory rate is 20, using some accessory muscles, but breath sounds are equal and normal on both sides.
Radial pulses are strong and equal. Skin is warm, pink, and dry. Capillary refill is less than two seconds.
I have an allergy to Dilaudid.
Are you currently taking any medications?
Cozaar, Advair, Crestor, Premarin, Singulair.
You mentioned Dilaudid allergy. Any other history to note?
I have a history of primary hypertension and osteoporosis.
Pulse is 90, regular and steady.
Respirations are 20, unlabored but using accessory muscles.
Blood pressure is 162 over 78.
Glucose could not be obtained at this time.
Scanning for deformity, crepitus, abrasions, or bleeding in head, neck, pelvis, and back-no findings at this time.
The pain is 10 out of 10. It feels constant and gets worse when I move or when touched.
Pupils are equal, round, and reactive, about 3 mm each.
Respirations slowed to 16, regular and consistent.
Blood pressure is now 147 over 87.
SpO₂ is up to 100%.
EKG is showing sinus rhythm.
Pulse remains at 90 and steady.
Administering 50 micrograms of Fentanyl via intranasal to help with the pain per protocol 3-10. It may take a few minutes to work.
Applying a 4-lead EKG monitor for continuous cardiac observation.
Attempting IV access in the left arm with a 22-gauge needle.
IV start was attempted in the left wrist with a 22-gauge needle but was not successful.
Confirming vitals again for transport accuracy. Everything looks stable at this point.
Continuing with Fentanyl administration according to protocol 3-10 for pain control.
Preparing a SCOOP stretcher for transfer to the main stretcher. You will be placed on your right side.
You'll be safely secured with a lap and shoulder belt for transport.
A second dose of 50 micrograms Fentanyl is now being administered while en route.
Blood pressure is 145 over 84.
SpO₂ is 98%.
Pulse is now 95, still regular.
Left hip remains very tender on palpation with no gross deformity or crepitus.
Final check of extremities and pelvis for DCAPBTLS-no new findings.
You are now en route to [COMPANY_NAME]. Your condition has stabilized.
IV was attempted in the left wrist with a 22-gauge needle. Access was unsuccessful.
Final vitals: pulse 95, respirations 16, BP 145/84, SpO₂ 98%.
You'll be taken to ED hall bed H at [COMPANY_NAME]. We alerted the hospital with a pre-arrival report.
Glucose was not obtainable at any point during initial or ongoing assessments.
Initially, SpO₂ was not obtainable at the time of the first assessment.
She remains alert, oriented to person, place, and time-still AOX4.
Rechecking for signs of pelvic injury-nothing observed at this point.
Direct transport to [COMPANY_NAME] was appropriate given hip pain and stabilized condition.
Securely loaded into ambulance and placed on cardiac monitor for ongoing monitoring.
Pulse: 95 with no sign of deterioration.
Respirations: 16, stable and consistent with time of last check.
Blood pressure 145/84, still steady.
SpO₂ at 98%, down from 100, but still well within acceptable range.
EKG continued to show a normal sinus rhythm.
No new signs of injury detected since last evaluation.
Pain control with Fentanyl was achieved with two initial doses.
Vitals remain the same. No new issues in visible range.
Engaging in distancing and communication from earlier scenes as no immediate issues identified.
You are very close to the hospital. Transport is expected to last under ten minutes.
Pulse remains 95 and consistent with previous values.
Respirations stable at 16 with reduced effort compared to initial arrival.
Blood pressure unchanged at 145/84.
Monitoring regularly and staying in communication with receiving hospital.
Hospital is aware of condition and protocol followed for handover.
Final check of all vitals and interventions complete-no further changes at this time.
We're approaching [COMPANY_NAME] and prepared for a smooth handoff.
Re-affirming that glucose has not been obtainable, and it doesn't appear to impact care at this time.
Hospital staff is aware of the patient's condition and history.
Transferring patient care to ED team-final handover details documented.
All interventions stayed within scope of protocol 3-10.
All patients are safe and care has been handed over.
EMTs documented all care rendered and communicated it to receiving providers.
Final report with patient vitals and conditions compiled for service records.
Entire care management has followed protocol with no deviations noted.
All actions taken matched medical directives and documented well for reporting.
Vitals trended steady, responsive to intervention with only minor post-Fentanyl changes.
Patient is in a secure position and ready for transfer to the hospital bed.
All medications administered were performed successfully and documented for hospital reference.
4-lead EKG monitoring was delivered and status confirmed en route.
Reassessed one final time while en route-no signs of deterioration observed.
You arrived at the ED in a safe and stable condition.
Roadway was clear and trip duration was within expected time frame.
Handover with hospital staff is complete and patient is now in their care.
Let me know if you need further support as care is officially transferred.
No remaining issues on the line. All phases of care were completed successfully.
Strictly followed general pain control protocols through out the entire process.
All medical history including all medications and known allergies were accurately relayed.
Communication with hospital informed them of protocols followed and immediate patient history.
Patient was stabilized and received safe transport to destination as required.
Final documentation and scene rituals concluded for this case.
All actions completed to safe and rapid transport per directive.
This call can be officially closed at this time.
Transport documented and tagged as complete for review and records.
All transport responsibilities fully discharged.
Patient safely and loosely handed over to hospital care resources.
Call will be closed and fully detailed for internal records.
Patient is now receiving follow-up care at ED hall bed H.
EMT partners packed and detailed care for administrative processing.
This completes the care for this patient based on current protocol.
Scene wrapped and all interventions recorded for later audit.
No further action necessary for this call.
Call file finalized for upper level monitoring.
I am now signing off this call and will document my actions.
All done and ready for another dispatch.
